recommendations: Add a minimal smoke test suite for all generated MCP endpoints (import + basic call + serialization)
pin and verify runtime dependencies by syncing `mcp_output/requirements.txt` with `pyproject.toml`/`requirements.txt` and adding a lockfile
fix repository metadata detection in the analyzer (`has_requirements_txt`/`pyproject` are incorrectly false) to improve generation reliability
add schema-validated request/response models per endpoint (especially for `Structure`/`Vasprun`-heavy objects) with clear error messages
implement endpoint-level timeouts and input size guards for large files/objects to prevent runaway jobs
add structured logging and tracing (endpoint name
latency
exception type
payload size) plus a debug flag in `start_mcp.py`
create a CI workflow for the MCP plugin (lint
type-check
smoke tests
package check) independent from upstream pymatgen CI
add contract tests for network-backed endpoints (`MPRester`
`OptimadeRester`) with mocked and live-optional modes
introduce caching for expensive/repeated computations (phase diagram construction
neighbor finding) with configurable TTL
expose only stable high-value methods first and mark risky/complex classes as experimental to reduce maintenance burden
improve `README_MCP.md` with concrete example payloads
failure cases
and per-endpoint performance notes
add version compatibility checks at startup (pymatgen/API major versions) and emit actionable upgrade guidance
harden adapter import fallback paths and add graceful degradation when optional deps (`mp-api`
`vtk`
`openbabel`
etc.) are missing
enforce consistent naming conventions for endpoints (`phase_diagram` vs `phasediagram`) and include alias mapping for backward compatibility
add payload normalization utilities for common crystallographic formats (CIF/POSCAR/JSON) to reduce client-side friction
include benchmark tests on representative large fixtures from `test-files` to set baseline latency/memory budgets
add security safeguards for file/path inputs (path traversal checks
allowed extensions
max bytes)
generate and publish an OpenAPI/JSON-schema-style capability manifest for MCP clients
split very large adapter/service modules into domain-focused submodules for maintainability
add deprecation policy and changelog specifically for MCP plugin releases

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize)
Resilience mechanism worked: clone failure was mitigated by zip fallback import (504 files discovered)
MCP plugin scaffolding was generated with valid entrypoints and package structure
Generated MCP service passed health/startup checks (stdio transport healthy)
Import-mode adapter strategy matched repository characteristics (high import feasibility 0.91)
failure_reasons: Primary Git clone failed due to network timeout to github.com:443 (curl 28)
Analyzer metadata extraction appears inconsistent (requirements/pyproject falsely detected as absent despite presence in file tree)
Quality signals are partially non-informative (0 LLM calls, 0 files processed, 0 LOC metrics), reducing confidence in measurement fidelity
Original project tests were not actually executed despite reporting fields (passed=false, execution_time=0, empty test files)

node_performance.download_time: Dominant latency contributor likely in download stage due to 75s clone timeout before fallback; recovery succeeded but increased end-to-end duration.
node_performance.analysis_time: Analysis completed functionally (core modules, dependencies, endpoints identified), but structured analyzer outputs were partially incorrect.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation succeeded quickly enough to complete full workflow in ~289s; produced expected MCP file set and endpoint list.
node_performance.test_time: Plugin smoke/health checks passed, but comprehensive functional tests were effectively absent (no endpoint-level invocation evidence).

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0 MB). Add real process-level measurement.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0%). Instrument per-node CPU stats.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Moderate footprint expected (504-file source snapshot + generated plugin), but reported generated size=0 indicates metrics pipeline issue.
